title: Houstonia terrellii
intro: Houstonia terrellii, also known as Terrell's bluet, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Houstonia terrellii include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Houstonia terrellii include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin conditions, and other health concerns.
taxonomy: Houstonia terrellii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a perennial herb that is native to the southeastern United States. The plant is often classified in the genus Houstonia, which is a group of small, flowering plants commonly known as bluet. Houstonia terrellii is a rare species that is typically found growing in woodland habitats. It belongs to the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the gentians and the coffee family.
